docs.text: Streets of Rage New Era - Lua Scripting API ¶ Welcome to the Lua Scripting API documentation for Streets of Rage New Era! This documentation provides information on how to create and modify game logic using Lua scripts. Whether you want to add new moves, create custom enemy AI, or manipulate game objects, you can do it with Lua. 🛠️ Key Features ¶ Full Control : Manipulate players, enemies, and items directly. Easy Integration : Seamlessly call game functions and access engine properties. Rapid Iteration : Modify scripts without recompiling the game. Friendly Syntax : Uses a simple, snake_case mapping from the C# engine. 📂 Where to find scripts? ¶ Scripts are typically located in the Scripts folder of your content pack. For example: Editor/Your Pack Name/Scripts/MyAwesomeScript.lua 🚀 Up Next ¶ Getting Started - Learn the basics, units, and how to create your first script. API Reference - Deep dive into the game , character , player , and enemy objects. Examples - Real-world scripts for blocking, projectiles, and AI targeting.
docs.title: Home

docs.text: Welcome to the Lua Scripting API documentation for Streets of Rage New Era! This documentation provides information on how to create and modify game logic using Lua scripts. Whether you want to add new moves, create custom enemy AI, or manipulate game objects, you can do it with Lua.
docs.title: Streets of Rage New Era - Lua Scripting API

docs.text: Full Control : Manipulate players, enemies, and items directly. Easy Integration : Seamlessly call game functions and access engine properties. Rapid Iteration : Modify scripts without recompiling the game. Friendly Syntax : Uses a simple, snake_case mapping from the C# engine.
docs.title: 🛠️ Key Features

docs.text: Scripts are typically located in the Scripts folder of your content pack. For example: Editor/Your Pack Name/Scripts/MyAwesomeScript.lua
docs.title: 📂 Where to find scripts?

docs.text: Getting Started - Learn the basics, units, and how to create your first script. API Reference - Deep dive into the game , character , player , and enemy objects. Examples - Real-world scripts for blocking, projectiles, and AI targeting.
docs.title: 🚀 Up Next

docs.text: Character API ¶ Every Character is built on top of an Item . It adds concepts of life, damage, and combat relationships. Characters include both Players and Enemies . They inherit all properties and methods from the Item API . Properties ¶ Property Type Description is_player bool True if this character is a player. is_enemy bool True if this character is an enemy. display_name string The name shown in the UI. health int Current health points. maximum_health int Maximum health points. lives int Current number of lives. score int Current score (Players only). victim Character The character currently being attacked by this one. attacker Character The character currently attacking this one. damage_taken int Amount of damage received in the last hit. combo_count int Current hit combo count. combo_reset_timer int Frames remaining before the combo resets. is_hitting_someone bool True if an attack is currently connecting. is_vaulting bool True if the character is vaulting over an opponent. is_blocking bool True if the character is in a blocking state. Methods ¶ check_being_hit_or_grabbed() ¶ Checks if the character is currently being hit or grabbed. check_being_hit() ¶ Checks if the character is currently being hit. check_being_grabbed() ¶ Checks if the character is currently being grabbed. check_hitting_entities() ¶ Checks if the character's attack hitboxes are colliding with any victims. try_grab(target, check_vicinity, enforcement, snap_to_victim) ¶ Attempts to grab another character. - target : The character ID or object to grab. - check_vicinity : If true, only grabs if nearby. set_animation(id) ¶ Changes the character's current animation. play_sound(pack_name, sound_name, channel) ¶ Plays a sound effect from a specific content pack. spawn_explosion(offset_x, offset_y, mode, direction) ¶ Spawns an explosion effect at the specified offset. try_spawn_shuriken(off_x, off_y, vel_x, vel_y, lifetime) ¶ Specifically for ninja characters, spawns a shuriken projectile. distribute_score(score) ¶ Distributes a specified score among all active players. distribute_score_based_on_damage(damage) ¶ Calculates and distributes score based on damage dealt to an enemy.
docs.title: Character

docs.text: Enemy API ¶ An Enemy is a specialized Character that is controlled by AI logic. It contains and extends all properties and methods from the Character API . Properties ¶ Property Type Description is_alive bool True if the enemy is active and has health remaining. mode int The current AI state. mode_timer int Time remaining in the current AI state. moving_direction int Direction the enemy intends to move. distance int Target distance from the player to maintain. movement_speed int Base movement speed of the enemy. enemy_status EnemyStatus Enum representing the overall status of the AI. enemy_agressiveness int How aggressively the enemy attacks (higher = more frequent). homing_target_id int ID of the specific player this enemy is targeting. homing_timer int Time remaining before the enemy switches targets or retargets. Character-Specific AI Values ¶ Some enemies have specific logic attached to these variables. The values can be overridden to change behavior patterns. Property Type Description enemy_specific_c0 int AI logic parameter 1. abadede_specific_c1 int Logic parameter used specifically by Abadede. abadede_specific_c2 int Logic parameter used specifically by Abadede. enemy_specific_c3 int AI logic parameter 2. enemy_specific_c4 int AI logic parameter 3. enemy_specific_counter_c5 int Counter used in specific enemy modes. shiva_specific_b0 int Logic parameter used by Shiva. biker_special_counter_cb int Counter used by bikers. is_biker_riding_bike_ca bool True if the biker is currently on a motorcycle. use_biker_pipe bool True if the biker intends to swing a pipe. Probability & AI Overrides ¶ These properties are used by the AI engine to decide what to do next based on the enemy data. Property Type Description override_ai_data_e0 int Manual override parameter 1. override_ai_data_e1 int Manual override parameter 2. override_ai_data_e2 int Manual override parameter 3. override_ai_data_e3 int Manual override parameter 4. override_ai_data_e4 int Manual override parameter 5. pack_name string The name of the content pack this enemy belongs to. Read-Only AI Mode Configuration ¶ These properties represent the raw AI data from the game's configuration files (often extracted directly from ROM mapping). Property Type Description probability_mode_e0 int Chance to switch to AI Mode 0. min_timer_mode_0_e6 int Minimum time spent in AI Mode 0. random_timer_mode_0_e7 int Random variance for AI Mode 0 timer. min_speed_mode_1_ea int Minimum speed while in AI Mode 1. random_speed_mode_1_eb int Random variance for AI Mode 1 speed. Methods ¶ return_to_standing() ¶ Resets the enemy to its default standing state. Enemies also rely on the inherited methods from Character and Item for actions like checking hits, setting animations, and applying throw damage.
docs.title: Enemy

docs.text: Game API ( game ) ¶ The game object is the global entry point for all game logic scripts. Properties ¶ Property Type Description current_frame int The current frame of the game engine. global_timer int The total number of elapsed frames since the start. level_id int The numerical ID of the current level. scene_id int The numerical ID of the current scene/wave. is_in_slow_motion bool True if the game is currently in slow motion. slow_motion_frames_left int Remaining frames for the slow motion effect. is_current_player bool True if the current script context is a player. is_current_enemy bool True if the current script context is an enemy. number_of_active_enemies int Count of enemies currently active in the scene. number_of_alive_enemies int Count of enemies that are still alive. number_of_free_sub_items int Number of available sub-item slots. this_character Character The character (Player or Enemy) currently running this script. this_player Player The player currently running this script (null if not a player). this_enemy Enemy The enemy currently running this script (null if not an enemy). this_item Item The item currently running this script. Methods ¶ get_player(id) ¶ Returns a Player object by its entity ID. get_enemy(id) ¶ Returns an Enemy object by its entity ID. get_character(id) ¶ Returns a Character object (Player or Enemy) by its entity ID. get_item(id) ¶ Returns an Item object by its entity ID. get_sub_item(id) ¶ Returns a SubItem object by its entity ID. get_free_enemy(consider_players_count) ¶ Returns the first available enemy slot. get_free_item() ¶ Returns the first available item slot. get_free_sub_item() ¶ Returns the first available sub-item slot. get_pack_hash(pack_name) ¶ Returns the numerical hash of a content pack name. set_clock(seconds) ¶ Sets the level timer to the specified value in seconds. set_slow_motion(frames) ¶ Activates a slow-motion effect for a specified duration in frames. reset_clock() ¶ Resets the level timer to its initial value. get_potential_score() ¶ Calculates the current total score, including any end-of-level bonuses. update_alternative_palette() ¶ Updates the alternative palette for specific objects based on game rules. schedule_go_forward_effect() ¶ Triggers the "GO!" indicator on screen. schedule_music(music_name, pack_name) ¶ Schedules a background music track to be played. If pack_name is omitted, the current game pack is used. is_next_wave_non_scrolling() ¶ True if the next wave of enemies won't require screen scrolling. get_number_of_active_players() ¶ Returns the number of players currently active in the game. kill_all_enemies(exception_id) ¶ Immediately defeats all active enemies, optionally excluding one by its ID. advance_level() ¶ Immediately advances the game to the next level. advance_wave() ¶ Advances the game to the next wave of enemies.
docs.title: Game

docs.text: Item API ¶ An Item is a physical object in the world. Every item contains a SubItem structure for visual representation. It adds physics, positions, and physical statuses. Properties ¶ Property Type Description position_x int X coordinate in the world (multiplied by 1000). position_y int Y coordinate in the world (multiplied by 1000). velocity_x int Current horizontal velocity. velocity_y int Current vertical velocity. impulse_x int Applied horizontal impulse. impulse_y int Applied vertical impulse. is_in_the_air bool True if the item is not touching the ground. is_active bool True if the item is currently active in the game. is_obstacle bool True if the item acts as a physical barrier. has_weapon bool True if the character is currently holding a weapon. weapon Item Returns the Item object for the weapon currently held. is_vulnerable bool True if the item can be hit. is_knocked_down bool True if the item is in a knocked-down state. was_hit_from_right bool True if the last hit came from the right side. Methods ¶ get_nearest_opponent(in_same_line, forward_check, backward_check) ¶ Finds the closest enemy relative to this item. try_spawn_item(pack, name, anim, mode, off_x, off_y, vel_x, vel_y) ¶ Spawns a new item into the world. Useful for projectiles or dropped items. update_position_and_check_world_collisions() ¶ Updates the item's position based on velocity and handles wall/floor collisions. copy_position_to(other_item_id, offset_x, offset_y) ¶ Synchronizes another item's position with this one.
docs.title: Item

docs.text: Player API ¶ A Player is a specialized Character that is controlled by a human input. It contains and extends all properties and methods from the Character API . Properties ¶ Property Type Description is_alive bool True if the player has lives remaining or health. player_index int The zero-based index of the player. input_state int The raw bitmask of the current input state. Button Held States (Continuous) ¶ These properties are true as long as the player is holding the respective button down. Property Type Description is_holding_up bool True if Up is being held. is_holding_down bool True if Down is being held. is_holding_left bool True if Left is being held. is_holding_right bool True if Right is being held. is_holding_forward bool True if holding the direction the player is facing. is_holding_backward bool True if holding the direction opposite to facing. is_holding_button_a bool True if the Special button (A) is held. is_holding_button_b bool True if the Attack button (B) is held. is_holding_button_c bool True if the Jump button (C) is held. is_holding_button_x bool True if the X button is held. is_holding_button_y bool True if the Y button is held. Button Pressed States (Just Pressed) ¶ These properties are true only on the exact frame the button was pressed. Property Type Description pressed_up bool True if Up was just pressed. pressed_down bool True if Down was just pressed. pressed_forward bool True if the forward direction was just pressed. pressed_backward bool True if the backward direction was just pressed. pressed_button_a bool True if the Special button (A) was just pressed. pressed_button_b bool True if the Attack button (B) was just pressed. pressed_button_c bool True if the Jump button (C) was just pressed. Timers & Combo States ¶ Property Type Description back_attack_pressed bool True if a back attack was triggered (usually B+C). is_special_attack_ready bool True if the player has enough health/meter to use a special. special_spam_timer int Buffer timer for special attack button. attack_spam_timer int Buffer timer for normal attack button. jump_spam_timer int Buffer timer for jump button. hold_direction_frames int Counter of frames a direction button has been held. Methods ¶ has_input_combo(combo_string) ¶ Checks if the player recently inputted a specific sequence of commands (e.g., "F, F, B"). - combo_string : The input string to check for. check_grab_while_moving() ¶ Handles logic for grabbing enemies while walking into them. safe_land() ¶ Returns the player to a standing state, typically used after recovering from a hit in the air. respawn() ¶ Respawns the player in the current scene.
docs.title: Player

docs.text: SubItem API ¶ SubItems are the base components of all game objects, handling animations and basic properties. Properties ¶ Property Type Description name string The internal name of the entity. pack_name string The name of the content pack this entity belongs to. is_enabled bool True if the object exists and is enabled. is_facing_right bool The horizontal orientation of the sprite. direction_signal int Returns 1 if facing right, -1 if facing left. palette_pack int The numerical hash of the content pack for this palette. palette_line int The color palette index currently in use. next_animation_id int ID of the next animation scheduled to be played. next_animation_frame int The frame index to start the next animation from. current_animation_id int ID of the currently playing animation. current_animation_frame int The current frame index within the animation. is_animation_over bool True if the current animation has finished playing. object_mode int The internal logic mode of the object. Methods ¶ set_animation(id, start_frame) ¶ Sets the current animation and optionally the starting frame. play_sound(pack, name, channel, pitch) ¶ Plays a sound effect attached to this object. clear_referenced_items() ¶ Clears any items currently linked or held by this object.
docs.title: SubItem

docs.text: SubItems are the base components of all game objects, handling animations and basic properties.
docs.title: SubItem API

docs.text: Property Type Description name string The internal name of the entity. pack_name string The name of the content pack this entity belongs to. is_enabled bool True if the object exists and is enabled. is_facing_right bool The horizontal orientation of the sprite. direction_signal int Returns 1 if facing right, -1 if facing left. palette_pack int The numerical hash of the content pack for this palette. palette_line int The color palette index currently in use. next_animation_id int ID of the next animation scheduled to be played. next_animation_frame int The frame index to start the next animation from. current_animation_id int ID of the currently playing animation. current_animation_frame int The current frame index within the animation. is_animation_over bool True if the current animation has finished playing. object_mode int The internal logic mode of the object.

docs.title: clear_referenced_items()

docs.text: Scripting Examples ¶ Here are some real-world examples taken from the Test chronicle pack to help you understand how to use the API. Custom Blocking Logic ¶ This script implements a manual block that reduces damage and handles knockback. -- Block.lua character = game.this_character if (character.check_being_grabbed()) then character.is_blocking = false return end character.is_blocking = true -- Reduce damage if hit while blocking if (character.is_being_hit) then character.damage_taken = character.damage_taken / 6 if (character.damage_taken < 1) then character.damage_taken = 1 end -- Manual knockback character.impulse_x = 196 character.is_facing_right = character.was_hit_from_right character.health = character.health - character.damage_taken -- Clear hit status so we don't fall down character.is_being_hit = false character.is_knocked_down = false end Spawning Projectiles ¶ Example of Ryu spawning a Hadoken fireball at a specific animation frame. -- Ryu Hadoken.lua local character = game.this_character if (character.current_animation_frame == 12) then -- packName, entityName, animationId, mode, offX, offY, velX, velY character.try_spawn_item("Test chronicle", "Ryu", 47, -1, -2750, -2750, -250, 0) end return true AI & Targeting ¶ Joe Musashi checking for the nearest opponent to decide between a melee attack or a shuriken. -- Joe Shuriken.lua local character = game.this_character if (character.current_animation_id == 29 and character.current_animation_frame == 0) then -- Check for someone nearby in the same line local nearest_opponent = character.get_nearest_opponent(true, true, false) if (nearest_opponent ~= nil) then local distance = math.abs(character.position_x - nearest_opponent.position_x) -- If too close, switch to a different animation (melee) if (distance < 3000) then character.set_animation(30) character.play_sound("Test chronicle", "kunai_hit", 2) return true end end -- Otherwise, throw shuriken character.try_spawn_item("Test chronicle", "Shuriken", 0, -1, -750, 3000, 524, 0) end
docs.title: Examples

docs.text: Getting Started ¶ Learn how to create your first script and understand the basics of the Lua integration. Basic Structure ¶ A Lua script is a text file with the .lua extension. The game executes these scripts when certain events occur (like an animation frame or a move update). The entry point for a script is its main body. Most scripts return true to indicate success. -- My first script local character = game.this_character if (character.is_hitting_something) then character.play_sound("Test chronicle", "impact", 1) end return true The game Global ¶ The game object is the main entry point to the engine. It provides access to the current frame, players, enemies, and items. game.this_character : Returns the character currently executing the script (either a player or an enemy). game.this_player : Returns the current player (if the script is running for a player). game.this_enemy : Returns the current enemy (if the script is running for an enemy). Object Hierarchy & Containment ¶ In Streets of Rage New Era , game objects are built in layers. A high-level object like a Player is not just a player; it is a character that is also a physical item and an animatable sprite. This is achieved through a "containment" model where each layer wraps the one below it: Player or Enemy (The Logic Layer) ⬇️ contains a... Character (The Combat Layer) ⬇️ contains an... Item (The Physics Layer) ⬇️ contains a... SubItem (The Visual Layer) Why does this matter? ¶ Understanding this hierarchy helps you find where specific properties and methods are defined: SubItem (The Foundation) : Handles the "visual" layer. Animations, palette lines, sprite orientation ( is_facing_right ). Basic sound playing. Item (The Physical Layer) : Handles "physics" and world state. Position ( position_x , position_y ), velocity, and impulses. Statuses like is_vulnerable , is_knocked_down , or is_active . Character (The Entity Layer) : Adds gameplay logic. Health, lives, and display names. Combat states like combo_count , victim , and attacker . Methods for grabbing, hitting, and specialized movement. Player & Enemy (Specialized Logic) : Player : Adds input handling ( is_holding_button_b , has_input_combo ). Enemy : Adds AI logic ( mode , enemy_agressiveness , distance ). Accessing Properties (Flattening) ¶ For your convenience, properties are "flattened". This means you can access properties from a lower layer (like Item or SubItem ) directly on a higher one (like Player or Character ) without having to go through a child property. local player = game.this_player -- These are all equivalent and perfectly valid: player.position_x = 5000 -- Accessing Item property through Player (Flattened) player.item.position_x = 5000 -- Accessing Item property explicitly player.character.item.position_x = 5000 -- Full explicit path Note : Properties like health are found at the Character level, while position_x is at the Item level. Because a Player contains a Character, you can just use player.health . Entity IDs (Global Indices) ¶ In the engine, every active object has a unique Entity ID (also referred to as object_index ). This is a global integer that the engine uses to keep track of every player, enemy, item, and sub-item in the scene. ID Ranges ¶ The IDs are organized in contiguous ranges. While the exact starting numbers can vary depending on the game configuration, the order is always the same: Players : Usually the first IDs (e.g., 0 to 3). Enemies : Follow the players. Items : Follow the enemies (weapons, containers, etc.). Sub-Items : The final range of IDs. Working with IDs vs. Objects in Lua ¶ The API often provides two ways to access related entities: by ID (integer) or by Object (shortcut). 1. Using Objects (Direct Access) ¶ Properties like character.attacker or character.victim are shortcuts. They automatically resolve the internal ID and return a full Lua object you can use immediately. local character = game.this_character local attacker = character.attacker -- Returns a Character object (or nil) if (attacker ~= nil) then -- Direct access to properties if (attacker.health < 50) then character.play_sound("MyPack", "taunt", 0) end end 2. Using IDs (Manual Resolution) ¶ You can work with the Entity IDs directly, for example requesting the attacker_character_id and obtaining the respective character object using game.get_character(id) method. local character = game.this_character local attacker_id = character.attacker_character_id -- Returns an integer ID if (attacker_id >= 0) then -- Convert the ID into a usable Character object local attacker = game.get_character(attacker_id) -- Now you can access its properties attacker.health = attacker.health - 10 end Important Units ¶ Positions : In-game positions are multiplied by 1000 for precision. When setting position_x , remember that 1 pixel is roughly 1000 units. Animations : Animation IDs are divided by 2 in Lua (mapping back to the engine's internal representation). Just use the IDs as they appear in the Character Editor.
docs.title: Getting Started
